title: proptest-cycle-rejection: add_dep_edge never lands a cycle
body:
# Goal

Assert that `Storage::add_dep_edge` never lands an edge that would close
a `Blocks`-or-`ParentChild` cycle — including mixed-kind cycles where the
existing edges are one kind and the new edge is another. Holds under
in-flight retries (lost-update window).

Would have caught the regression class behind `121f48b` (mixed-kind cycle
silently accepted) and `43c7615` (single-kind cycle accepted) cleanly,
without depending on the harm-class attack tree to surface it.

# Approach

- Use `multi_issue_repo_strategy` (from `proptest-multi-issue-generator`) to spin up N issues.
- Generate a sequence of `add_dep_edge` calls. After each one, walk the post-state dep graph and assert no `Blocks`/`ParentChild` cycle exists. Failed `add_dep_edge` calls (cycle-introducing) must return typed `Error::Invalid` and leave the graph untouched.
- Stretch goal: a "concurrent two-writer" variant that lands two cycle-half edges in interleaved order to simulate the lost-update window. May require a small test harness on top of `add_dep_edge`; punt to a sub-follow-up if it's too heavy.

# Why now

The cycle-detection codepath is exactly the kind of "looks correct, but a subtle path lets one through" that proptest catches better than hand-attack. Two real bugs (`121f48b`, `43c7615`) in this surface in the last sweep — durable defense matters.

# Blocked by

- `proptest-multi-issue-generator`.

# References

- Parent: `df74809` (qa-proptest-harness), commit `28e5ed71`.
- Bugs this would have caught: `121f48b`, `43c7615`.
- Existing cycle-rejection: `0f2fb9db`.

# Acceptance

- New property `add_dep_edge_never_closes_cycle` ships green at 16 cases.
- Stress-runs clean at `PROPTEST_CASES=256`.
- Failed cycle attempts: `Error::Invalid`, source graph atomic.
